structured.summary: The paper proposes a novel inter-sentence pretraining task called Text Structure Prediction (TSP) that leverages text structure information to improve language model pretraining, demonstrating enhanced performance across various model scales compared to existing methods.

strengths.point: The paper introduces a novel task, TSP, which incorporates text structure information into language model pretraining.
strengths.evidence: Section 1 Introduction and Section 3 Task Design

strengths.point: The proposed method shows improved performance across different scales of language models.
strengths.evidence: Abstract and experimental results in Section 3

strengths.point: The approach is versatile and can be combined with existing word-level pretraining tasks with minimal additional computational cost.

weaknesses.point: The paper lacks a detailed analysis of why existing inter-sentence tasks fail at larger scales.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 1 Introduction mentions the failure but does not provide an in-depth analysis.

weaknesses.point: The experimental setup and results are not fully detailed, making it difficult to assess the robustness of the findings.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 3 mentions experiments but lacks comprehensive details on datasets and metrics.

weaknesses.point: The paper does not provide a clear comparison with state-of-the-art methods beyond the mentioned baselines.
The scalability of the TSP task in terms of computational resources is not thoroughly discussed.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 3 mentions minimal additional costs but lacks detailed analysis.

weaknesses.point: The paper could benefit from more extensive ablation studies to isolate the effects of different components of the TSP task.

questions.question: Can the authors provide more details on the datasets and evaluation metrics used in the experiments?
questions.motivation: Understanding the experimental setup is crucial for assessing the validity and reproducibility of the results.

questions.question: How does the TSP task perform in comparison to other state-of-the-art methods not mentioned in the paper?
questions.motivation: A broader comparison would help in understanding the relative performance of the proposed method.

questions.question: What are the specific computational costs associated with implementing the TSP task at large scales?
questions.motivation: Understanding the computational requirements is important for practical applications of the method.

critique_points.point_001: The paper lacks a detailed analysis of why existing inter-sentence tasks fail at larger scales. (Section 1 Introduction mentions the failure but does not provide an in-depth analysis.)
critique_points.point_002: The experimental setup and results are not fully detailed, making it difficult to assess the robustness of the findings. (Section 3 mentions experiments but lacks comprehensive details on datasets and metrics.)
critique_points.point_003: The paper does not provide a clear comparison with state-of-the-art methods beyond the mentioned baselines. (Section 3 Experimental Results)
critique_points.point_004: The scalability of the TSP task in terms of computational resources is not thoroughly discussed. (Section 3 mentions minimal additional costs but lacks detailed analysis.)
critique_points.point_005: The paper could benefit from more extensive ablation studies to isolate the effects of different components of the TSP task. (Section 3 lacks detailed ablation studies.)
critique_points.point_006: Can the authors provide more details on the datasets and evaluation metrics used in the experiments? (Understanding the experimental setup is crucial for assessing the validity and reproducibility of the results.)
critique_points.point_007: How does the TSP task perform in comparison to other state-of-the-art methods not mentioned in the paper? (A broader comparison would help in understanding the relative performance of the proposed method.)
critique_points.point_008: What are the specific computational costs associated with implementing the TSP task at large scales? (Understanding the computational requirements is important for practical applications of the method.)
